Best overallNinja Creami NC301 Ice Cream Maker$undefined$229.99AmazonLearn more
Best compact ice cream makerCuisinart FastFreeze Ice Cream Maker$97.56$119.95AmazonLearn more
Best for soft serveGreenPan Frost Ice Cream & Frozen Drink Maker$363.76$399.99AmazonLearn more
Best for traditional ice creamCuisinart Ice Cream Maker$59.95$89.95AmazonLearn more
Want more from NBC Select? Sign up for our newsletter, The Selection, and shop smarter.
Ninja Creami versus Cuisinart, KitchenAid, Nutribullet and GreenPan

Below are descriptions of our experience testing the ice cream makers from popular kitchen brands and comparing them to the Ninja Creami. Aside from being common competitors for the Ninja Creami, many of these ice cream makers have at least a four-star average rating from 500 or more reviews on Amazon.
Ninja Creami NC301 Ice Cream Maker
Easy to set upMany pre-set recipesWorks fast after freezing
Easy to set up
Many pre-set recipes
Works fast after freezing
It’s loud
It’s loud

Ninja sent me the Creami to test for this story, which I chose over the Deluxe since it has more reviews overall. This was the first appliance that I tested and I used the ice cream recipe that comes in the included recipe booklet, which has a cream cheese base (instead of the common egg base for traditional ice cream). Aside from that, I did a simple vanilla ice cream recipe with heavy cream, sugar, whole milk and vanilla extract. Like with most at-home ice cream makers, the Ninja Creami requires you to freeze the ice cream base at least 24 hours ahead of time to ensure that it reaches the right texture when you actually make it. It also comes with pints and a mixing bowl with a handle, both of which are dishwasher safe.

After testing it myself, sharing some of my creations with my coworkers and comparing it to the appliances below, I can confirm that the Ninja Creami really does live up to the hype and it is definitely a 9.9 out of 10 for me (I took points off for loudness). It was easy to set up and wash all of the pieces ahead of time, thanks to the quick-start guide and, after freezing it for a day, the ice cream was ready in around 10 minutes.

The texture of the ice cream was creamy and smooth, even with an egg-free, cream cheese base that doesn’t require any tempering (increasing the temperature of beaten eggs and then adding them to the warm ice cream base). It was also one of only two machines that I found had the closest texture to store-bought ice cream. It’s very loud, though, comparable to a blender or vacuum. But that shouldn’t be too much of a problem if you don’t have to make ice cream when people around your home are sleeping.
Cuisinart FastFreeze Ice Cream Maker
Small sizeEasy to storeVery lightweight
Small size
Easy to store
Very lightweight
Requires the most manual labor
Requires the most manual labor

Cuisinart sent me this appliance, which recently launched, and I was excited about it because I was already planning on testing the brand’s popular traditional ice cream maker, which has a different setup. This is much more lightweight and more compact compared to every other ice cream maker I tested.

While this ice cream maker has multiple pre-sets similar to the Ninja Creami and GreenPan Frost (reviewed below), it has a somewhat different process for making ice cream. While you still have to freeze the base ahead of time (I used the same cream cheese base recipe without any tempering or heat), this one has a handle on the top that you push up and down slowly as the machine runs. It takes more manual labor than any other machine I tested.

My colleagues and I were surprised by the results in the best way — it was almost identical in texture, taste and creaminess to the Ninja Creami. It was much smoother than I expected, since it didn’t have any eggs, and it didn’t have an icy texture that sometimes happens with homemade ice cream recipes. Some of my coworkers actually preferred this one over the Ninja Creami, though I believe they were both delicious, with this one being a very close second place for me. Aside from the fact that the ice cream came out great, it also was made in a small and lightweight appliance that is less than half the price of the Ninja Creami.
While I don’t think this is better than the Creami, per se, I do think it’s a great alternative for someone who wants a compact appliance, like apartment dwellers and those low on space.
KitchenAid Cream Maker Attachment
Easy to installEasy to storeLightweight
Easy to install
Easy to store
Lightweight
Requires freezing for 24 hours
Requires freezing for 24 hours

KitchenAid sent Malin this ice cream maker attachment to test, courtesy of the brand. As an avid baker, she uses her KitchenAid Stand Mixer often, so setting up the ice cream maker bowl was simple. Unlike the Ninja Creami, Cuisinart FastFreeze and the GreenPan Frost, this ice cream maker is just an insulated bowl with non-toxic freezing liquid that helps keep the bowl and ice cream cold when it’s mixing.

Malin made the ice cream base a day ahead before installing the bowl to the mixer and says it was easy and fast to do. “I love making homemade ice cream, but I can’t fit another appliance in my tiny kitchen, so being able to add this attachment to my KitchenAid and turn it into an ice cream maker solves my problem,” she says.

The biggest drawback to this pick is the obvious: It’s an attachment to the KitchenAid mixer, so if you don’t already have one, you can’t use it. We did think it was worth testing since the stand mixer is so popular; anyone considering putting this on their wedding registry, housewarming wishlist, or just considering splurging on it for the summer should know what they’re getting.

Overall, this was my third place choice for all the ice cream makers. The texture was great but it doesn’t have as many pre-sets as the Creami or FastFreeze and it requires a separate appliance. It’s not better than the Ninja Creami, but it’s perfect for anyone who already has a Kitchenaid Stand Mixer and wants to make their own ice cream.
Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker
Simple setupMakes a large portionAffordable
Simple setup
Makes a large portion
Affordable
Doesn’t have pre-sets
Doesn’t have pre-sets

I made ice cream with this ice cream maker a year or so ago, but the brand sent me a courtesy one to refresh my memory. Similar to the KitchenAid Stand Mixer, this style of ice cream maker is much more simple in design and doesn’t have pre-sets for soft-serve, sorbet or similar, just traditional ice cream. The appliance includes an insulated bowl lined with a freezing liquid that you have to freeze a day in advance, a base that rotates the bowl with the ice cream base, a paddle and a cover.

I’ve made both traditional ice cream with a tempered egg base and a version with a cream cheese base, and overall, while both were delicious, I found that this appliance is best for traditional ice cream, which has eggs in it. The freezing method and the paddle help the ice cream achieve a great creamy texture that isn’t icy and is easy to scoop. Compared to the other ice cream makers on this list, the paddle is goes further into the freezer base, which I believe helps make the texture creamier.

After freezing the bowl for a day and then preparing the base, it took about 20 minutes for the ice cream to start forming. Overall, I believe this is the easiest of all the appliances to use, so it’s great for anyone who usually bypasses most of the instructions since it’s quite intuitive to use.
Compared to the Creami, it doesn’t have any pre-sets or customizations for texture, but it makes for the best egg-based ice cream.
GreenPan Frost Ice Cream & Frozen Drink Maker
Doesn’t require freezingMakes a large portionEasy to clean
Doesn’t require freezing
Makes a large portion
Easy to clean
It’s heavy
It’s heavy

This is one of the newest appliances on this list, so I was excited to try out a courtesy sample, which the brand sent me. I love GreenPan because of its non-toxic cookware, including its popular Stanley Tucci collection, which I’ve had for years and has held up very well. This appliance makes soft service ice cream, sorbet, milkshakes and other frozen drinks, like margaritas or frosés.

I tested the ice cream function of this machine and it didn’t take long to set up or start using because it doesn’t require freezing in advance. This ice cream maker has a built-in feature that uses electricity to freeze the ingredients as it turns in the bowl. This is easily the best feature of the appliance if you prefer soft serve over traditional ice cream, and I also love the old-fashioned style of the lever on the front.

What I also love about it is that it has a dial on the front that, after selecting the pre-set you want, you can use to customize the texture. Additionally, while the freezer bowls for some of the ice cream makers above aren’t dishwasher safe, the parts on this machine are not only dishwasher safe, but it also has a feature that removes excess residue while you add soap and water to it as an alternative way to clean it. This was easy to do and didn’t require me to use a sponge or anything.

Compared to the Ninja Creami, the Frost makes much softer ice cream, so it’s not necessarily better, but it’s best for people who love soft serve or fro-yo. With that said, I love that it doesn’t require any freezing ahead of time, which all the others do.
Nutribullet Chill Ice Cream Maker
Small sizeEasy to useHas multiple pre-sets
Small size
Easy to use
Has multiple pre-sets
Best for fruit-based mixes
Best for fruit-based mixes

While I love my Nutribullet personal blender, which has helped me make amazing salsas, smoothies and protein shakes, this was my first introduction to their recently launched frozen treat appliance. The brand sent Malin a courtesy sample of the ice cream maker to test for this story, and she made two types of bases: a dairy-based vanilla ice cream mixture and a mixture made with frozen mango.

The machine works similarly to the personal blenders: It includes two 16-ounce cups with lids that you invert onto the base with the mixing blade. When you turn on the machine, the blade moves up and down to mix the ice cream or sorbet base, but this is more automatic than manual. After testing both the ice cream and sorbet mixture, Malin and I both felt like the mango-based sorbet turned out the best texture-wise, while the ice cream, while delicious, was very soft in texture, similar to soft serve, which I don’t mind (though I like regular ice cream more).

“It’s a little cheaper than the Creami and it’s more compact in size, more lightweight and quieter than the Creami,” says Malin. The sorbet came out great in the Chill but the ice cream was much softer compared to the Creami, so it’s only better if you prefer sorbet or fruit-based frozen desserts.
How I tested the ice cream makers against the Ninja Creami
There are a few key considerations that went into testing and the criteria I used to judge each of the ice cream makers:

Setup: As with any kitchen appliance, the first thing that you consider is how easy (or intimating) it is to follow the directions on the instruction manual to make sure you use it correctly without breaking it or getting bad results. Luckily, all of the ice cream makers above were relatively easy to set up. Outside of rinsing the removable parts before the first use, there wasn’t much else to do aside from preparing the base in advance (for all of them except the GreenPan Frost). The Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker was the easiest overall — it literally only has an on/off button on the front and that’s all you have to know after freezing the bowl ahead of time. While the GreenPan Frost doesn’t require freezing in advance, it does have slightly more parts to clean before first use compared to the others. Ease of use: Similar to setup, all of the ice cream makers were easy to use after a few reads of the instruction manual, but the appliances I found to be the most streamlined were the Ninja Creami and the Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker. The Ninja Creami’s quick-start guide is very helpful in understanding how to use it the first time and in addition to having many pre-sets, it also has a re-spin function that lets you add more milk or liquid while mixing to help you achieve the texture you prefer, which I loved. While the Cuisinart FastFreeze came out with one of the best textures, it does require you to push the handle down slightly to mix the ice cream, so I’d get this for someone young moving into their first (small) apartment instead of an elderly relative. Ice cream texture: The most important factor in all of this testing is how the actual ice cream came out. I wanted to see which ice cream maker made something that felt the most similar to traditional egg-based ice cream and/or which one had the creamiest texture that didn’t have tiny unblended pieces of ice or graininess. Overall, all of the ice cream makers delivered delicious flavor, though some of the textures, such as from the GreenPan and Nutribullet, were much more similar to soft serve (the GreenPan one makes it clear that it’s best for this style) while the Ninja Creami and the Cuisinart FastFreeze had the best texture to me, though the ice cream from the original Cuisinart was also great.

Setup: As with any kitchen appliance, the first thing that you consider is how easy (or intimating) it is to follow the directions on the instruction manual to make sure you use it correctly without breaking it or getting bad results. Luckily, all of the ice cream makers above were relatively easy to set up. Outside of rinsing the removable parts before the first use, there wasn’t much else to do aside from preparing the base in advance (for all of them except the GreenPan Frost). The Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker was the easiest overall — it literally only has an on/off button on the front and that’s all you have to know after freezing the bowl ahead of time. While the GreenPan Frost doesn’t require freezing in advance, it does have slightly more parts to clean before first use compared to the others.

The Ninja Creami’s quick-start guide is very helpful in understanding how to use it the first time and in addition to having many pre-sets, it also has a re-spin function that lets you add more milk or liquid while mixing to help you achieve the texture you prefer, which I loved. Overall, all of the ice cream makers delivered delicious flavor, though some of the textures, such as from the GreenPan and Nutribullet, were much more similar to soft serve (the GreenPan one makes it clear that it’s best for this style) while the Ninja Creami and the Cuisinart FastFreeze had the best texture to me, though the ice cream from the original Cuisinart was also great.
How to decide which ice cream maker is best for you
To decide which of the above ice cream makers is best for you, here is a brief breakdown of what I believe is each appliances’ best attributes, plus some drawbacks to keep in mind.

Ninja Creami Ice Cream Maker: This appliance is highly versatile and customizable and creates great, creamy texture. It also comes with a helpful recipe guide. That said, it is the loudest of all the appliances, so be sure your family members and neighbors don’t mind. Cuisinart FastFreeze Ice Cream Maker: This is the smallest of all the appliances I tested and would be great for someone with a small apartment, and it has multiple pre-sets. This ice cream maker, however, requires manual effort, so this is great for someone fresh out of college or simply willing to do the arm workout in exchange for the lower price. KitchenAid Cream Maker Attachment: If you’re an avid home baker and enjoy making ice cream every once in a while, this is the best option for you. I also think that since it works with a versatile appliance like the KitchenAid Stand Mixer, it’s also great for small apartments, though this means you must own a KitchenAid Stand Mixer. Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker: This is the best option for anyone who loves ice cream just the way it is, wants it to be the most similar to a store-bought tub, and doesn’t plan on making many custom mixes. I also believe this is the best option for people who aren’t active or skilled in the kitchen since it’s so simple to use. With that said, it doesn’t have any pre-sets or ways to customize the texture. GreenPan Frost Ice Cream Maker & Slushie Machine: If you and your family prefer soft serve ice cream or frozen yogurt over traditional ice cream, this is the best option for you. It’s also great for anyone who loves frozen beverages like slushies, milkshakes and margaritas. Nutribullet Chill Ice Cream Maker: This is a great option for people who prefer to make soft sorbets or fruit-based frozen treats like açai bowls or sherbet. It’s also compact and great for small apartments, similar to the brand’s personal blenders.

Ninja Creami Ice Cream Maker: This appliance is highly versatile and customizable and creates great, creamy texture. It also comes with a helpful recipe guide. That said, it is the loudest of all the appliances, so be sure your family members and neighbors don’t mind.

This ice cream maker, however, requires manual effort, so this is great for someone fresh out of college or simply willing to do the arm workout in exchange for the lower price.

KitchenAid Cream Maker Attachment: If you’re an avid home baker and enjoy making ice cream every once in a while, this is the best option for you. I also think that since it works with a versatile appliance like the KitchenAid Stand Mixer, it’s also great for small apartments, though this means you must own a KitchenAid Stand Mixer.

Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker: This is the best option for anyone who loves ice cream just the way it is, wants it to be the most similar to a store-bought tub, and doesn’t plan on making many custom mixes. I also believe this is the best option for people who aren’t active or skilled in the kitchen since it’s so simple to use. With that said, it doesn’t have any pre-sets or ways to customize the texture.

GreenPan Frost Ice Cream Maker & Slushie Machine: If you and your family prefer soft serve ice cream or frozen yogurt over traditional ice cream, this is the best option for you. It’s also great for anyone who loves frozen beverages like slushies, milkshakes and margaritas.

Nutribullet Chill Ice Cream Maker: This is a great option for people who prefer to make soft sorbets or fruit-based frozen treats like açai bowls or sherbet. It’s also compact and great for small apartments, similar to the brand’s personal blenders.

Overall, after trying all of the ice cream makers on this list, I enjoyed the Ninja Creami the best because the ice cream itself came out great — the texture was perfect and even though it requires freezing ahead of time, it mixes the ice cream up very fast. It also has a re-spin feature that allows you to adjust the texture of the ice cream and re-mix it. Plus, it has many pre-sets for gelato and sorbet.

A very close second for me was the Cuisinart FastFreeze, which is less expensive than the Ninja Creami and the ice cream had an almost identical texture. Plus, it has multiple pre-sets and is much more compact, which I loved.
Frequently asked questions

There are alternatives to the Ninja Creami that are both cheaper and more expensive, but whether or not they are “better,” depends on your preferences. If you want an ice cream maker that’s small, a great alternative would be the Cuisinart FastFreeze. If you prefer soft serve ice cream and don’t like having to freeze ice cream mixes ahead of time, then the GreenPan Frost is a suitable option. If you want the most traditional, store-bought style ice cream and don’t mind prepping food ahead of time, the Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker is a fit. For people who prefer sorbet over ice cream, the Nutribullet Chill Ice Cream Maker is an excellent choice.
Why trust NBC Select?

I’m a commerce editor at NBC Select, where I cover home and kitchen appliances, cleaning, organization and more. I’ve written stories about air fryers, coffee makers and vacuums, as well as shower cleaning products and more. I’ve also written about food and kitchen appliances for more than eight years and have previously worked as a food editor at multiple food magazines. For this story, I tested courtesy ice cream makers from several popular brands, with help from my colleague Zoe Malin (she tested two), who also writes about kitchen appliances for NBC Select.

title: Deal of the Day: Birkenstock Arizona sandals are 40% off
summary: Podiatrists say that Birkenstock Arizona sandals are some of the most comfortable shoes on the market thanks to the built-in arch support. Right now, get them for 40 percent off.
story: Once June hits, you will almost always find me in a pair of Birkenstocks. As someone who has flat feet and a history of foot pain, I find them to be the most reliable shoes when it comes to all-day comfort. My favorite style from the brand is their Arizona sandal, and right now the Birkenstock Arizona Big Buckle Sandal in white is 40 percent off.

Experts have told NBC Select editors on multiple occasions that they are some of the most comfortable sandals on the market. If I had to list a drawback for these shoes, there would only be one — they’re pricey. So I’ll gladly take a discount on any color, especially the perfect summer white.
Deal of the day
Birkenstock Arizona Big Buckle Sandals
Podiatrists have previously recommended Birkenstock Arizona sandals to us, saying they’re great for those with flat feet or for anyone looking for comfortable walking sandals. Here’s why:

They have a contoured footbed with built-in arch support and a molded heel cup to help keep your foot in place as you walk. The two wide straps are also adjustable to give you a customizable fit, which helps to ensure the sandal stays on, even if your feet get a little clammy from summer heat. The adjustability also scores points in the summer, when your feet may swell and you need a looser-than-normal fit. The sole is made from a pliable cork-latex material that provides a good mixture of rigidity and plushness for comfortable support.

Currently, only the white version of these shoes is 40 percent off, but there are also a number of other Birkenstocks on sale (with several colors available) on the brand’s website, with prices as low as $74.

title: Stephen Colbert Signs Off from ‘The Late Show’ In Last Episode Tonight
summary: Here is when the final episode of “The Late Show with Stephen Colbert” will air and how you can watch it.
story: The curtain is coming down on “The Late Show with Stephen Colbert.” The final episode of the long-running late-night TV series is set to air.
Colbert announced during an episode in July 2025 that CBS canceled the show after 33 years on the air.
“Before we start the show, I want to let you know something that I found out just last night,” he said. “Next year will be our last season. The network will be ending the ‘Late Show’ in May.”
The audience booed at the news, prompting Colbert to continue, “Yeah, I share your feelings. It’s not just the end of our show, but it’s the end of ‘The Late Show’ on CBS.”
Colbert further said a new host was not coming in to replace him, but “this is all just going away.”

Colbert, who was honored by fellow late-night hosts Jimmy Fallon, Jimmy Kimmel, Seth Meyers and John Oliver when they appeared on his show May 11, has served as host of “The Late Show” since 2015. He took over for David Letterman, who created the show and had hosted since it premiered in 1993.
When is the final episode of “The Late Show with Stephen Colbert”?
The final episode will air May 21 at 11:35 p.m. ET.
In a move of solidarity, the late-night programs hosted by Jimmy Fallon on NBC and Jimmy Kimmel on ABC will be dark as Colbert takes his final bow.
How can I watch the final episode of “The Late Show with Stephen Colbert”?
All episodes of the show air on CBS and stream the next day on Paramount+.
Who will be the guests on the final episode of “The Late Show with Stephen Colbert”?
Guests for the final episode have not been announced in advance. However, CBS has been promoting the finale as an “extended” episode, which likely means the show will run beyond its hour-long format.
What will take the place of “The Late Show with Stephen Colbert”?
With the show coming to an end, “Comics Unleashed,” hosted and produced by Byron Allen, will take over the 11:35 p.m. ET time slot on CBS beginning May 22.
Back-to-back half-hour episodes will air each night Monday through Friday. That will be followed by back-to-back episodes of the comedy game show “Funny You Should Ask,” beginning at 12:35 a.m. ET.
What will Stephen Colbert do after “The Late Show”?
Colbert, a well-known fan of “The Lord of the Rings,” will write a new movie based on parts of “The Fellowship of the Ring” that have not yet made it to the big screen.
“I will see you all in the shire,” Colbert said while announcing the news in March with Peter Jackson, who directed “The Lord of the Rings” and “The Hobbit” trilogies.

title: David Letterman Reacts to the End of Stephen Colbert’s ‘Late Show’: ‘It’s a Huge Mistake’
summary: David Letterman believes the end of “The Late Show” on CBS is a “huge mistake” and a loss for American culture.
story: David Letterman believes the end of “The Late Show” on CBS is a “huge mistake” and a loss for American culture.

The legendary former “Late Show” host shared his take with TODAY’s Craig Melvin on the cancellation of the CBS late-night program after 33 years on the air. The final episode with host Stephen Colbert airs May 21.
Letterman, 79, who hosted the show from its inception in 1993 until 2015, spoke with Craig from the iconic Indianapolis Motor Speedway in his native Indiana.

“Kind of makes me sad,” Letterman said. “We always relied on, you would read the newspaper in the morning, and at night you would see Johnny Carson. And Johnny would give you a perspective whether you agreed with it or not, but it was always funny.
“And without that, I think we’re losing a valuable perspective. I think it’s very, very important to the American culture.”

Colbert, who took over for Letterman in 2015, announced in July 2025 that CBS was canceling the show. The heads of Paramount Global and CBS released a statement, saying their “agonizing decision” was “purely a financial” one and is “not related in any way to the show’s performance, content or other matters happening at Paramount.”

News of the cancellation came just days after Colbert called CBS’ $16 million settlement with the White House over a “60 Minutes” interview with then-Vice President Kamala Harris a “big, fat bribe” on “The Late Show.”
The show is being replaced with “Comics Unleashed,” hosted by Byron Allen.
“I think it’s too bad that Stephen is gone,” Letterman told Craig. “I think it’s a huge mistake.”
Letterman also appeared on “The Late Show” with Colbert on April 15 and talked about the cancellation.
“As we all understand, you can take a man’s show, you can’t take a man’s voice, so that’s the good news,” he told Colbert.

Craig spoke with Letterman at Indianapolis Motor Speedway because it’s tied to a cause close to Letterman’s heart. The diehard racing fan, who co-owns Rahal Letterman Lanigan Racing with legendary Indianapolis 500 champion Bobby Rahal and businessman Mike Lanigan, has joined a campaign to urge racing fans to get screened to avoid heart valve failure.

RLL Racing has partnered with Edwards Lifesciences for the “Stay In the Race” campaign to set up heart valve screening booths at races around the country, emphasizing the importance of early detection and timely referral among adults 65 and older.
Letterman, Rahal and Lanigan have all survived serious heart scares.
“I feel great,” Letterman said about his health. “I love the topic of this, and I love heart maintenance in particular.”
Letterman had quintuple bypass surgery in 2000, Rahal has had quadruple bypass surgery and Lanigan has undergone triple bypass surgery.
The trio are featured in a short documentary called “Stay in the Race” that will premiere online on May 22.
“It could be a silent killer,” Lanigan said. “And if you go out, get your heart valves checked, and if you’re OK, then you’re having a great day. If you find out you have an issue, they can fix it.”

title: Who Won ‘Survivor’ Season 50? A Recap of the Finale
summary: Read a recap of the “Survivor 50” finale and find out who won the $2 million prize. Plus, see who was awarded Sia’s $100,000 fan-favorite prize.
story: Warning: Major spoilers ahead for the “Survivor 50” finale…

“Survivor”’s historic Season 50, which brought together fan-favorites who have captivated audiences across the show’s 26-year run, has come to an end, with one player walking away a whopping $2 million richer.

“Survivor 50” began with 24 former castaways arriving on the beaches of Fiji and sizing up their competition. From legends like five-time players Cirie Fields and Ozzy Lusth to other highly- anticipated returnees like Christian Hubicki, Mike White and Season 45 winner Dee Valladares, the cast was stacked with strategic masterminds and competition beasts. Throughout the season, the players had to navigate new twists that saw multiple contestants getting their torches snuffed in some episodes. They also had to adapt to celebrities like Jimmy Fallon and Billie Eilish impacting the game.

After managing to outwit, outlast and outplay some of the best “Survivor” players of all time, only five castaways remained when the epic three-hour finale kicked off on Wednesday, May 20: Aubry Bracco, Jonathan Young, Tiffany Ervin, Joe Hunter and Rizo Velovic.
The stakes couldn’t be higher thanks to an appearance from MrBeast and a risky move by castaway Rick Devens in Episode 10 that raised the prize winnings to $2 million.

This year, the beloved live after show returned. But in a change to the format, host Jeff Probst checked in with eliminated contestants in segments throughout the night, resulting in one awkward mistake that had the cast and fans at home in disbelief.

In between the conversations, viewers learned which three contestants made it to the Final Tribal Council after surviving the last two Immunity Challenges and the Fire-Making Challenge. So, who claimed the title of “sole ‘Survivor” at the end of the milestone season?
Read a recap of the “Survivor 50” finale, including a breakdown of the after show where singer Sia awarded one player $100,000 for being the biggest fan-favorite, below.
1st Immunity Challenge

The finale, titled “Reverse the Curse,” begins with the final five returning to camp. Rizo celebrates his “blindside” of Cirie and points out in his confessional that he is guaranteed to make it to the final four thanks to his Hidden Immunity Idol.

Tiffany calls out Aubry’s “fake sympathy” after keeping her out of the vote and planning to get rid of her in the previous episode. As Tiffany voices her frustrations to the cameras, the other players criticize her and plot their next move.

At the first Immunity Challenge of the night, Tiffany, Joe and Jonathan are all neck and neck while placing the last pieces in their puzzles. But Jonathan pulls out the victory and Probst places the Immunity Necklace around his neck for the second time this season, securing his place in the final four.
1st Tribal Council

Back at camp, Rizo tells Tiffany that he is voting her out and there is nothing she can do to change his mind. So, Tiffany turns her focus elsewhere and tries to convince Joe and Jonathan that Aubry is a bigger threat due to her “underdog story.”

At Tribal Council, Tiffany points out how frustrating it was to sit at camp and listen to other players say she was a competition threat even though she has won the same amount of challenges as Joe. Despite her convincing argument, the remaining players are not receptive.

Rizo plays his Hidden Immunity Idol and Tiffany is voted out. She becomes the 20th person voted out and the 10th member of the jury.  “I was seen as one of the biggest threats on the beach. It just lets me know that, I guess, I’m a ‘Survivor’ giant, too,” she says in her final confessional.
2nd Immunity Challenge

Before heading to the second Immunity Challenge in the episode, Rizo reads a scroll to the group that doesn’t reveal how the rest of the season will end, whether in the divisive Fire-Making Challenge or a vote to determine the finalists.
The final four arrive at their last Immunity Challenge of the season and learn they will compete in “one of the most iconic challenges” in the show’s history: “Simmotion.”
Jonathan is the first out and is shortly followed by Rizo, leaving Aubry and Joe. Aubry outlasts Joe and wins her first Immunity Challenge of the season, making her the first finalist.
“It doesn’t feel real,” she tells Probst before he places the necklace around her neck.

Probst then announces that fans voted prior to the season to have a Fire-Making Challenge instead of a vote, so Aubry will determine who is going to the end with her and which two players have to make fire.
Fire-Making Challenge

Aubry chats with Joe and swiftly decides that she will take him to the end. Rizo and Jonathan, who both lost their Fire-Making Challenges on their prior seasons, prepare to go head-to-head and redeem themselves.

Aubry announces her decision to Probst and the jury at Tribal Council. Rizo and Jonathan get ready to take their spots but then the show oddly cuts back to the live portion. Probst welcomes Rizo to the stage and asks him if he has any regrets on his game. Rizo says “no,” and then Probst, mistakenly, invites him to take a spot next to the other jurors as “the final member of our jury.”

Rizo and the rest of the cast look stunned and the audience is silent, realizing Probst accidentally revealed the Fire-Making Challenge result. “What just happened?” Probst asks before the show cuts to a commercial.
Back from commercial, Probst jokes that his error is the last “twist” of the season and now we will see Rizo lose to Jonathan.
With all the anticipation gone, viewers see Jonathan build a strong fire and win the challenge. Rizzo then, officially, becomes the 21th person voted out and the 11th and final member of the jury.

Rizo recalls watching “Survivor” as an 8-year-old child and says that unfortunately his story will be “falling short both times” he played. He becomes emotional as he says he represented his Albanian community, inspired people, made his family proud and played the best game he could.
Final Tribal Council

Before Final Tribal Council, the finalists are surprised with visits from their loved ones: Joe’s wife, Jonathan’s brother and Aubry’s mom. The three enjoy breakfast with their families and predict how the end game will unfold.
Finally, the grilling from the jury begins. Here is what some jury members asked:

Benjamin “Coach” Wade: What is a core value from home that you carried with you into the game?Rick: Were there any secrets you had in this game that you kept? If not, what was the strategy behind not keeping any secrets?Stephenie LaGrossa Kendrick: Does not ask a question but praises Jonathan for using old school and new school strategies. She calls him “the total package.” Ozzy: How did you adapt during the game?Emily Flippen: What is one critique of the game you played for Season 50?Christian: What is your story this season that should make you a winner?
Benjamin “Coach” Wade: What is a core value from home that you carried with you into the game?
Rick: Were there any secrets you had in this game that you kept? If not, what was the strategy behind not keeping any secrets?
Stephenie LaGrossa Kendrick: Does not ask a question but praises Jonathan for using old school and new school strategies. She calls him “the total package.”
Ozzy: How did you adapt during the game?
Emily Flippen: What is one critique of the game you played for Season 50?
Christian: What is your story this season that should make you a winner?
And the Winner Is…
Probst thanks the castaways for a great season and reveals that fans want the winner to be announced live in Los Angeles at the after show.
Continuing tradition, the host carries the urn with the votes onto the stage and joins the contestants live in front of the studio audience.
“It is time to bring this season to a close,” he says as the crowd cheers.
Probst reads the votes and declares Aubry the winner of Season 50 by a vote of eight to three.
Reflecting on her victory, Aubry says, “I came back and I was more intuitive. I trusted myself and I moved differently. And most importantly, I learned from all the players before me.”
As the runner-up, Jonathan says he is proud of his game and that advice from Rob “Boston Rob” Mariano helped him strategize before playing.
Joe says he was a fan of the twists this season because it “put everyone on their heels.”
Probst then reveals that in addition to the $2 million, Aubry is also being gifted a Toyota Land Cruiser.
Sia’s Fan-Favorite $100,000 Prize Winner

To end the episode, Probst mentions Sia being a “super fan” and says the singer asked viewers to vote for a player to win her $100,000 fan-favorite prize. He says Cirie was the clear winner and will receive the Sia prize.
Highlights from the Live After Show
Cirie is given the “Spirit of Survivor Award” early in the night “for inspiring others to discover the fire that burns within.”

Mike White later appears virtually from France as he is busy filming Season 4 of “The White Lotus.” During his chat with Probst, Mike reveals that Season 50 contestants Charlie Davis and Kamilla Karthigesu are going to make cameos in the next season of his HBO hit.
In addition to Probst’s blunder with Rizo, he also catches up with Christian, Rick Devens, Ozzy, Stephenie and Tiffany.
Then, fans are briefly introduced to players they can expect to see on Season 51, which ushers in the “Open Era.” Probst teases “dangerous new elements never seen before” coming next season.
He says viewers can look forward to “permanent uncertainty” in the next wave of the show, coming this fall.

title: Why Are Harry Styles’ Fans Complaining About His Tour?
summary: Some Harry Styles fans are complaining about obstructed views, seats and the stage during his new tour. Read his team’s response.
story: Harry Styles has officially kicked off his worldwide “Together Together” tour, which includes a 30-date residency at Madison Square Garden in New York City. However, some fans are taking issue with the stage layout.

The Grammy winner’s first “Together Together” concert, featuring songs from his new album “Kiss All The Time. Disco, Occasionally,” was on May 16 in Amsterdam, followed by a May 17 show in the same city.

Ahead of his third show out of six at Johan Cruijff Arena, Styles’ team spoke out to address fans’ complaints regarding the stage design, which obstructed views in certain sections, specifically the VIP and general floor areas.
What Are Fans Saying About Harry Styles’ Tour?
One fan who attended the May 16 show, shared a picture of her limited view when opener Robyn was on stage.
“Just seen Robyn pop up on a screen....no idea where she is oh,” X user Kirsty wrote. However, she did share additional photos of Styles up close and personal, writing she “had the best time.”

Another fan who also saw the opening night show tweeted, “why does VIP front GA have reduced visibility? We can’t see anything of the main stage.” The fan described the view as a “total scam and false advertising.”

One X user shared a photo from their eye level and warned that if “you’re short you won’t see the main stage from the rear GA front row.” They also clarified that that was not their section. The fan purchased the tour package that included circle pod seats and appeared to have a good view of Styles from their spot.
There was enough chatter online about the staging issue that the singer’s team commented on the situation.
What Did Harry Styles’ Team Say?

On Harry Styles HQ’s Instagram story May 20, his team noted the less-than-perfect experiences some fans have had during the first two shows in a statement. They added that they are “actively working” to make adjustments and improvements.

“We’ve heard concerns from some fans regarding sightline obstructions on the floor. We want every person in the room to have the best experience possible, and we are actively working on making adjustments to improve visibility, while keeping everyone’s safety a priority,” the statement said.
The statement added that beginning May 20, “the front bridges will be altered in Amsterdam and London.”

“For future venues, we are working as quickly as possible to make adjustments that also fit within safety code and local compliance. In the meantime, temporary barricade adjustments have been made to the left and right front GA pits for tonight’s show to improve stage visibility,” it continued.

His team thanked fans for their “patience and understanding, and for being part of together, together with us.” The statement concluded with a “Dance No More” reference: “We love dancing with all our friends.”

Styles will continue his “Together Together” tour in Amsterdam, with a total of six shows, followed by stops in London, São Paulo and Mexico City, before his MSG residency and several concerts in Australia begin.

title: Jenna Bush Hager Gives Impassioned Rant Against Book Banning on ‘Las Culturistas’ Podcast
summary: Don’t get Jenna Bush Hager started about the increasing number of books being challenged and banned at libraries.
story: Don’t get Jenna Bush Hager started about the increasing number of books being banned from libraries.

The TODAY co-host and creator of the Read With Jenna book club had a passionate reaction to book banning on the May 20 episode of the “Las Culturistas” podcast with Bowen Yang and Matt Rogers, which she expanded upon on TODAY.

In a recurring segment on the show called “I Don’t Think So, Honey,” guests are encouraged to give a short rant about a topic they are passionate about. Jenna chose the skyrocketing amount of attempts to have books banned and removed from school and public libraries.
“I don’t think so, honey. Why are we banning all these books, y’all?” Jenna said on the show. “Why are we banning the ‘Wicked’ book? Why are we banning ‘To Kill a Mockingbird’?
“I posted a picture of my daughter reading ‘The Summer I Turned Pretty’, and you know what people said to me? They shamed me,” Jenna continued. “I don’t think so, honey.”
Jenna noted how kids have access to all types of content on YouTube through iPads and phones, yet certain books are being targeted so children can’t read them.
“Leave it up to the librarians,” she said on the podcast. “I want my kid to read. Want to know why? Books start conversations. Know what we’re not having enough of in this country? Conversations!”

Jenna has long spoken about how she inherited her love of reading from her mother, former first lady Laura Bush, and now she has worked to pass it on to her three children. Continued attempts to ban books have clearly struck a nerve.
She said on TODAY May 20 that her segment on “Las Culturistas” came from “somewhere really deep and guttural, and it came out like a word vomit.”

Jenna also reflected on the reaction to her allowing her then-10-year-old daughter, Mila, to read the young adult novel “The Summer I Turned Pretty” in 2024. Jenna posted a photo to her Instagram story of Mila reading the book with a friend while at the beach.

“People were like, ‘How could you let her read this?’” she said on TODAY. “First of all, I’m laying next to her when she reads it. I’m her mother. If she has questions, she’s going to come to me and ask it, and also y’all, your kids are getting on YouTube and have access to everything.

“It’s such an interesting world we live in where there’s very little regulation on the internet and everything is accessible, and we are regulating libraries,” she continued. “... I understand the mom that would say, ‘Well, this isn’t appropriate for my child.’ But that’s why we train librarians to know what is age appropriate, what is on-level for reading, and obviously I come from a mother who was a librarian, but what this does is to tell these central figures in our communities that they are not knowledgeable enough.”

The American Library Association’s Office for Intellectual Freedom recorded attempts to censor 4,235 unique titles at public and school libraries in 2025, which is nearly 2,000 more titles than in 2024 and five shy of the record set in 2023, according to the association’s 2026 report. The average number between 2001 and 2020 was only 273 before the challenges increased exponentially.

The ALA also found that 91.7% of the titles that were challenged in 2025 came from pressure groups (20.8%) and government decision-makers (70.9%), not concerned parents. Only 2.7% came from parents, according to the ALA’s report.

“The point is there are people that are trusted adults in the library that can decipher that, and parents as well, we have to take responsibility for our kids our home, but let’s not get rid of Toni Morrison’s books for the kids that do need to read that, that should be reading that,” Jenna said on TODAY.

“I might just go ahead and say I think it’s in the 90s percent of the complaints coming about these books are not coming from parents, they’re coming from these organizations, and I’m not with it,” she added. “I don’t think so, honey. Don’t ever get me started on that again.”

title: Everything to Know About John Travolta’s Daughter, Ella
summary: Who is Ella Bleu Travolta? Here’s what to know about John Travolta and Kelly Preston’s only daughter.
story: Talent runs in the Travolta family.

Ella Bleu Travolta, the only daughter of actors John Travolta and the late Kelly Preston, has been following in her parents’ footsteps since 2009, when she made her onscreen debut in the film “Old Dogs” at the tender age of 7.
Over the years, the father-daughter duo has collaborated on several projects, and they’re at it once again with their latest film, “Propeller: One-Way Night Coach.”
John Travolta wrote, directed and narrated the Apple TV movie, which follows a young airplane enthusiast’s big trip in the skies.
Before the film’s premiere on May 29, the Travoltas attended the Cannes Film Festival together to promote the project. There, John Travolta was surprised with an honorary Palme d’Or.
Ella Bleu Travolta looked stunning in a sleek black gown, and her father was dapper in a suit and a cream beret.
In April, John Travolta sent a sweet shoutout to his daughter  and shared a photo of her at an event.
“So proud of my baby girl @ella.bleu at the 2026 fashion US awards pink carpet. Look out for Ella in my directorial debut, ‘Propeller One-Way Night Coach,’” he captioned the post.
Read on to learn more about the entertainer the “Grease” legend admires so much.
She’s a Middle Child
After getting married in 1991, “The Experts” co-stars John Travolta and Preston welcomed their first child, a son named Jett Travolta, in 1992.
Flash forward eight years and they expanded their family with the addition of a daughter named Ella Bleu Travolta on April 3, 2000.

“To the sweetest girl in the world. You are magical in all that you do,” Preston wrote in a 2020 Instagram post devoted to her daughter. The sweet sentiment accompanied a throwback bath-time pic of the mother-daughter duo.

Of his daughter, John Travolta raved to People in 2019, “She is her own person. She is gracious, generous, poised, graceful and gorgeous. I don’t know how she came to be, and I don’t take any credit other than just adoring her. And maybe that’s a valid contribution.”

Jett Travolta, who’d had a history of seizures throughout his life, suffered a fatal one at the age of 16 in 2009 while staying at his family’s vacation home in the Bahamas. Following his death, John Travolta and Preston welcomed a son named Benjamin in 2010.
She Got Her Start Acting with Her Parents
Ella Bleu Travolta’s film career kicked off with the 2009 Disney comedy “Old Dogs,” which also stars both of her parents — and that was, of course, no coincidence.

Her dad first pitched the idea of her appearing on screen when she was just 5 years old, but as he once explained during a visit to “The Ellen DeGeneres Show,” she told him she wasn’t ready yet. When she changed her tune two years later, he wasted no time securing her a role in a film he’d been offered. And when Preston wanted to make it a family affair, he made that happen, too.

“It was the best filming experience I’ve ever had, bar none,” Preston said in an interview while promoting the film. “It was so much fun, and it was just the greatest to be able to be in her first movie.”
Ella Bleu Travolta went on to star alongside her dad once more in 2019’s “The Poison Rose,” before moving on to her first leading role in the “Alice in Wonderland” riff “Get Lost.”
In 2026, Ella Bleu Travolta also starred in “Alicia in Wonderland” and “Propeller: One-Way Night Coach,” per IMdB.
While speaking with People in 2023, Ella Bleu Travolta gushed about how much she enjoys working with her father.
“I always jump at the opportunity to do something with my dad and we always have the most fun doing something together,” she said.
While speaking with Parade in 2023, Ella Bleu Travolta said she’s wanted to follow in her parents’ footsteps for a long time.

“Pretty much as long as I can remember, I’ve always just loved the arts in general—acting, singing, dancing, and I grew up around it. So it was just such a natural part of my life that continuing to do that just feels right and I really enjoy it. It’s definitely been something that I’ve wanted to do for pretty much my whole life,” she said.
She Released Her First EP in 2024
In addition to being an actor, Ella Bleu Travolta is also a singer.

John Travolta, who’s recorded a number of celebrated albums over the course of his own career, encouraged his daughter to pursue her passion for music when he learned that she had written and recorded snippets of songs during the pandemic,.
“He was like, ‘Oh, wow, you should really finish these. You should finish these songs, and then you could record demos of them or something,’” Ella Bleu Travolta recounted to People.
After that, she released her first two singles: “Dizzy” in 2022 and “Little Bird” in 2024.
The budding musician’s father even co-produced her first EP, “Colors of Love,” which debuted in 2024.

“He’s amazing and he’s so protective because he’s been in this industry — in the movie industry, in the music industry — forever, so he knows how to protect me in that sense,” Ella Bleu Travolta explained during an appearance on the 3rd hour of TODAY.
She added that she’d love to do a Christmas duet with him in the future.
She’s Dabbled in Modeling
In addition to her work in the film and music industries, Ella Bleu Travolta has also tried her hand at modeling.
In 2022, John Travolta took to Instagram to share a photo of his daughter during fashion week.
“So proud of Ella’s debut at fashion week in New York City!” he captioned the post.
In 2025, Ella Bleu Travolta appeared on the “Tea Time” podcast and discussed her passion for fashion.

“I do love fashion. I’m definitely finding myself more and more in the fashion world. I really appreciate it. And I do love all the different styles and seeing people’s different styles. So, it’s really nice to sort of be getting to be a part of that a little bit,” she said.
Ella Bleu Travolta also explained how the fashion, movie and music industries are “very different” but “all go together.”
Looking to the future, Ella Bleu Travolta said she’d like to stay involved in the fashion industry in some capacity.
“I’d love to design some stuff someday, like have some collaboration. I think (it) would be really, really cool,” she said.
She Gets the Best Advice from Her Father
The career encouragement Ella Bleu Travolta has received from her father over the years is a two-way street.
“He’s my biggest fan, and I’m his biggest fan, so there’s a lot of support going on,” she explained in a chat with Parade in 2023. “It’s amazing. I learn so much from him, all the time.”
Ella Bleu Travolta told the publication her father always has the best advice, but he doesn’t want her to follow it blindly.
“So, whether I follow it or whether I make my own conclusion from it, he’s always encouraging me to think for myself,” she said.
She Loves Having a ‘Creative Outlet’
During her appearance on the “Tea Time” podcast in 2025, Ella Bleu Travolta discussed her love for both acting and singing.
“It would be great one day to sort of like merge the two, which is definitely possible,” she said.
The actor went on to say “all kind of art makes me happy.”
“I love painting as well. And as long as I have some creative outlet, I’m happy,” she said.
She Honors Her Late Mother and Brother Every Year
Ella Bleu Travolta’s family has been rocked by tragedy more than once.
In 2020, the family suffered another blow when Preston, at the age of 57, died following a battle with breast cancer.
One of the songs on her new EP, “Little Bird,” is a tribute to Preston, with its music video featuring family footage.

“I think it’s different for each person going through loss but for me personally it was just this pure message I wanted to sort of give to my mom, because even though there can be so much support from everyone it can also sometimes get in the way of how you truly feel and how you want to communicate those sort of last words to that person or just give a communication to them,” she said on the 3rd hour of TODAY in 2024.
“This was sort of my message from a baby bird to a mama bird and also to yourself too because you will always know what’s right,” she continued.

title: The Sweet Meaning Behind Prince Harry’s 8-Year Wedding Anniversary Gift to Meghan Markle
summary: Meghan Markle shares the sweet and personal wedding gift Prince Harry got her for their 8-year- anniversary.
story: Eight years ago, the world was invited to celebrate and watch as Prince Harry and Meghan Markle said “I do.”

And this year, the now-Duchess of Sussex shared intimate photos of her and her husband’s wedding day ceremony and reception. But, she also shared the 8-year wedding anniversary present that Harry surprised her with and the special meaning behind it.
On her May 19 Instagram story, Meghan first shared video of her two kids, Archie, 7, and Lilibet, 4, singing, “Happy anniversary to mama and papa.”
As Harry holds the cake and Meghan films, they share a kiss and she says, “I love you.”
A little one can be heard telling them to blow the candles.

In the next Instagram video, the former “Suits” star shows off the bronze penguins that Harry gifted her. Traditionally, bronze or pottery is given after eight years of marriage, making the Duke of Sussex’s surprise the perfect combination of sentimental and traditional.
Gasping as Harry giggles in the back, she says “Whaaat, oh my God!”

She then shares the story behind the penguins, telling her children, “When mama and papa, when we got engaged we had a party with all of our friends and we said everyone wear animal onesies and we were penguins ... because they’re together for life.”
Next to the bronze penguins was a photo of her and Harry in their onesies.
As Meghan showed off her personal and sweet wedding gift, there appears to be a poster made by the kids leaning up against the table.
The former actor continued to share snapshots from their celebrations at home, which also included enjoying a lemon elderflower cake.

American pastry chef Claire Ptak was in charge of baking the couple’s wedding cake, which, according to Kensington Palace at the time, was a lemon sponge cake drizzled with elderflower syrup that included Amalfi lemon curd and Swiss meringue buttercream.
Before digging into the delightful treat, the couple shared a kiss for the camera.
In another video, Meghan also shared a close up of her bronze penguins and a card that was addressed to “The one and only.”
She also made sure that everyone got a nice look at her and Harry  all dressed up at their engagement party.
The happy couple announced their engagement on Nov. 27, 2017, after dating for over a year.

They would go on to get married at St. George’s Chapel inside Windsor Castle in 2018 and welcome son Archie on May 6, 2019. The couple would go on to officially step back as senior members of the British royal family in 2020 and settle in Montecito, California, where they welcomed daughter Lilibet in June 2021.

title: Lindsay Hubbard on Her Daughter’s TV Debut, the ‘Summer House’ Reunion and Her Early Thoughts on Season 11
summary: “Summer House” star Lindsay Hubbard dishes to TODAY.com about the show’s spinoff, the three-part Season 10 reunion and her plans to meet up with Amanda Batula.
story: Lindsay Hubbard has a lot to talk about.

First, there’s the debut of Bravo’s new series “In the City,” which will see Lindsay, fellow “Summer House” vets and some newcomers showcasing their lives in New York. It premieres May 19, immediately following the Season 10 finale of “Summer House,” the Hamptons-centric show on which Hubbard has been a fixture since its inception in 2017.

Then, there’s the “Summer House” Season 10 reunion airing in three parts — on May 26, June 2 and June 9 — to an audience eager to see the fallout from the bombshell reveal by cast members Amanda Batula and West Wilson that they’ve struck up a relationship.

For the uninitiated: Amanda and West announced their “connection” on Instagram March 31 and sent shockwaves through the Bravoverse. This came two months after Amanda and Kyle Cooke, her “Summer House” co-star and husband of four years, announced their separation, and as viewers were watching Amanda’s best friend Ciara Miller and West, who are exes, rekindle their romance in new episodes of “Summer House.”

In the walk-up to those releases, Lindsay hosted an event centered on a topic that hits close to home: skin care for herself and her 1-year-old daughter, Gemma. Partnering with Aveeno Baby & Kids, Lindsay led a community walk with the brand in the Brooklyn borough of New York City on May 16.

“With Gemma, she has been accustomed from a very early age about skin care,” she tells TODAY.com. “I think that just as much as we as adults, as women, as moms really take care of our skin, I think that we should do that with our babies as well.”

Just before her community event with Aveeno, TODAY.com caught up with Lindsay to talk about the upcoming premiere of “In the City” and her daughter’s TV debut — and, yes, that “Summer House” scandal, too.
This interview has been condensed and lightly edited for clarity.
Oh, she’s on it, girl. She is on it. (Laughs) I feel like people have gotten lost in the sauce with this whole scandal lately, and I appreciate you actually like asking me about it.
I’m like, “Guys, Gemma’s on. Gemma’s going to be on. Like, hello!” She’s a star. She is the star of the show. It’s not me, it’s not Kyle. It’s Gemma.

She took her first steps on camera. Again, she’s a star. And yeah, she’s in it with me. “In the City” is about our lives in that next chapter and phase, right? ... It’s a lot of our friends who are in that next chapter where people are married. People have babies. People are trying to figure out if they should have babies. Like real life, adult conversations and decisions that need to be made. And you get to see a day-to-day inside look into my life.
You get to see Gemma, and it’s really sweet. I’m actually really excited to look at that footage back from the fall, because they change so much. I’m probably going to cry every episode.

People and fans and viewers have been really wanting to see more of our city life. They’ve been requesting it over the last couple of years. “Summer House” is a show based in the Hamptons, so, yes, we can go to lunches and dinners and events in the city, but this (show) is our entire lives in the city.

Fan and viewer request is our command here. So I think it’ll be fun. I also think it’s a niche age, right? That has been missing from reality television. We are in our 30s and 40s. We are growing up and making some of the biggest decisions of our entire lives in this moment, and you guys are going to see it all.
I feel like our group ... it’s almost like we need to get these cameras up every single day, because something new is happening every day.
Was I involved? Yes, I was in that pickup situation. There are multiple pickups, and we also have two different TV shows to toggle between.
So I don’t know where anything’s going or what’s going to happen. I don’t make those decisions. But yes, I’m obviously always very involved. I’m a very involved person.
I literally tell them all the time. I’m like, “Put a camera here, put a camera there, and just roll,” because the weirdest stuff goes down in this house.

I don’t have any expectations in these situations. I don’t know what to expect ever. Weird stuff happens in our world. I would say the filming was very intense. It was very dramatic. And it is going to be a fun watch for viewers.

I thought he did a great job on “Watch What Happens Live,” and I do agree with his characterization of the status of our friendship. I thought he did great. Even since the reunion — which, we filmed the reunion three weeks ago — I would say, we have been together more in doing different press. And we were at a friend’s birthday on Saturday. So we kind of just keep running into each other, depending on whatever we’re doing. I think that’s helpful.
But we have a very long overdue conversation on (the next) episode of “Summer House,” and that was sort of the starting point to rebuilding whatever it is that we’re rebuilding.

And I think that that was a much needed conversation. And now, ... it’s a slow build, but I would say we’re really friendly. We’re in a good place. I look forward to being around him now more than I have in the last couple of years.
You know, he met Gemma ... and she high fived him. We’re in a great place.

I don’t know what my goals are with the conversation. The whole thing is wild, right? Again, I don’t have a lot of expectations in my world. I just sort of take it at face value and see what happens and deal with it as it comes. With Amanda, we got really close over the last year — specifically the last year. And there were a lot of feelings on my side about this entire scandal and the decisions that she made.
I think that she also doesn’t have a lot of friends around her to tough love her. I think it’s more just sitting in a comfortable, intimate setting and making sure that I can ask more questions.
Girl, at this point, all my questions are equal. There’s a lot. There’s a lot of questions in there.

But, I mean, the reunion’s airing soon, and you guys are going to find out a lot there. And I think, this is more (of a step) toward continuing to find more out. Again, everything happens — something happens every single day. So it’s just, like, get the cameras on.

Great question. Upfronts was, like, three days ago. I mean, I’m sure I’m going to be around. I’m always around. I live in New York, I go to the Hamptons. ... I think we’re all just like, “OK, a new summer. A new season.” We’ll see what happens. I’m in it with you. I’m pretty much where you guys are, trying to figure it out.
But I’m not worried. I can tell you that. Certainly not worried.

title: The 2 Words Henry Winkler Lives By, According to the Heartfelt Commencement Speech He Just Gave
summary: Henry Winkler shared heartfelt wisdom with the Class of 2026 in an commencement speech at Emerson College.
story: Henry Winkler shared the two words he lives by and his heartfelt request for the Class of 2026 in a commencement speech at Emerson College.

The “Happy Days” legend and current History Channel host spoke on May 10 at his alma mater in Boston, where he graduated in 1967. He is one of several celebrities who have been delivering words of wisdom to graduating college students this spring.

He shared his struggles in high school, where he said he was “in the bottom 3% academically in America” before being accepted to Emerson out of the 28 colleges he applied to. Winkler, 80, spoke with TODAY.com in 2023 about growing up with dyslexia, which contributed to his struggles in the classroom but didn’t dim his hope of one day becoming an actor.
“But I had a dream, and I never let that dream out of my mind,” he said in his commencement speech.
He then shared what has fueled a legendary career in television and movies.
“I live by two words: tenacity and gratitude,” he said. “Tenacity will get you where you want to go, and gratitude will make you enjoy the journey, no matter how bumpy.”
Winkler also advocated for the graduates to trust their gut over anything else.
“You listen to your tummy — your tummy knows everything, your mind only knows a few things,” he said.
As they progress in their journeys after college, he also called for the graduates to put themselves in others’ shoes.
“You cannot live a rich life if you don’t have empathy,” he said.
Winkler concluded his speech with a rousing message.

“Every one of you is powerful,” he said. “Some you know your power. Some of you are scared of your power. Some of you haven’t touched it yet, you’re not aware it’s even there yet. And I want to tell you, you are powerful, and in you is a great gift and your job is to figure out what your gift is because this world needs every single one of you. And your job is to find that gift and give it to the world. It doesn’t matter what it is, we need it! Go!”

title: Emily Henry Addresses ‘Beach Read’ Casting Criticism
summary: Emily Henry has some words of wisdom for fans of her book “Beach Read” who are anxiously awaiting its screen adaptation
story: Emily Henry has some words of wisdom for fans of her book “Beach Read” who are anxiously awaiting its screen adaptation.

During an appearance on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle May 19, Henry discussed the upcoming movie based on her bestselling book and addressed reactions over the casting of Phoebe Dynevor and Patrick Schwarzenegger as its leading couple.
“I’m along for the ride with the readers, and we just have to trust the vision,” she said.
Dynevor was cast in February as the movie’s leading lady, January Andrews. In April, readers learned that Schwarzenegger would be starring alongside her as Augustus “Gus” Everett.

After the news broke, some of Henry’s avid fans criticized Schwarzenegger’s casting and took to X and TikTok with the hashtag, #NotMyGus. Some social media users argued he doesn’t seem to represent how Gus is described in the book: rugged, disheveled and brooding, with dark curly hair. Since the book’s publication in 2020, popular fan castings for the character have included Logan Lerman, Paul Mescal, Dev Patel and David Corenswet.
“I described it as like casting Barbie to play Wednesday Addams,” one TikTok user said in an April video.
Some, though, pointed to Schwarzenegger’s breakout role as the  the polished, fratty Saxon Ratliff as evidence of his acting range.

“A lot of people upset by this casting have forgotten about ‘The White Lotus’ Season 3 and how Patrick Schwarzenegger made us feel bad for the most unlikable character of the series and it shows,” one person wrote on Threads.
On TODAY, Jenna Bush Hager asked Henry about her avid fanbase, including how “some were happy with the news, some weren’t.”
“How do you as the writer just say, ‘This is going to be fine, guys’?” Jenna asked of the casting.
“I also have to sit back and let it unfold,” Henry responded. “It’s not — I mean, it is our baby, the readers’ and my baby — but it’s also the filmmakers’ baby and the studio’s baby.”
Henry, who said it’s “so surreal” to have her book adapted into a movie, encouraged readers to have faith in the movie’s production team.
“I feel like Yulin Kuang, our writer/director, is so brilliant, and I know she’s going to do an amazing job,” Henry said.
Looking ahead, Henry said she’s excited to see how filming progresses. For now, she’s content with knowing a few casting secrets before the rest of us find out.

“I also just keep getting emails about everyone else that they’re locking in, which is not public news yet, so I can’t share. But I’m getting really excited. They’re starting shooting very soon,” she said.

Kuang, who also wrote the screenplay for the film adaptation of Henry’s book “People We Meet on Vacation,” previously described Schwarzenegger as a “slow burn” as she recalled the casting process in an Instagram post.

“There was something about the way he talked about his feelings on love and romance that I couldn’t forget. But I was unconvinced we’d found our Gus, because I knew we had to be exhaustive in our efforts, given how important this character is to readers,” she said.
Kuang said the movie’s team saw “a lot of men for this role” and wanted to cast a “relationship” rather than “a character.”

“Once we knew Phoebe would be our January, my efforts became more focused and specific — not on who would play the best Gus according to the text, but who would play the best Gus opposite our specific January. That meant in-person director sessions and chemistry reads with Phoebe. The moment Patrick sat across from her, there was something electric in the room with us,” she said.
After the news of his casting broke, Schwarzenegger said on Instagram he’s so excited for the part.

“First — let me say thank you to @emilyhenrywrites for the opportunity!” he wrote. “I will do everything possible to make the fans happy & proud and bring your brilliant writing to life. PS- my wife is obsessed with you!”

title: Telemundo Host Has a Sweet Response After Viewers Sensed a ‘Spark’ Between Him and Shakira
summary: A Telemundo host responde after an interview between him and Shakira went viral because of a spark between the two of them.
story: Shakira’s hips don’t lie, and her lips might not either.
At least, that’s what the singer’s fans are suggesting after what they describe as a flirty interaction between the 49-year-old and Telemundo TV host Clovis Nienow.
The “Whenever, Wherever” singer sat down with the journalist while promoting her upcoming FIFA World Cup halftime performance.
Fans picked up on flirty vibes during the interview, which also featured FIFA President Gianni Infantino and Global Citizen CEO Hugh Evans.
At one point, Shakira licked her lips and smiled, prompting some social media users to pick up on romantic vibes between the two.
“That look of Shakira with the host... as if there was a spark 😂 😂 😂,” one Instagram user commented on a video of the interview.
“Shakira looking at Clovis like: 🫦,” another wrote.
“As if ‘Cupid’ was also invited to that interview. 😂💘,” one commented.
Some suggested that Shakira and Nienow should date.
“@clovisnienow and @shakira what a great couple they would make 💏🫶🏼,” one wrote.
After Nienow’s interview with Shakira went viral, the reporter addressed the speculation from viewers during a separate TV appearance.

“I don’t know about body language, but what I do know, and what I felt is that I’m incredibly grateful to Shakira and to Telemundo for giving me this interview opportunity,” he said, according to a translation by TODAY.com.
The reporter went on to describe Shakira as “admirable,” “charming” and “successful.”

“She is also a beautiful woman...I can’t deny that. When I had her in front of me, it was impossible not to admire her beauty …. but in that moment I was concentraing on the interview. We had a very short amount of time,” he said.
Nienow said he had more questions to ask the singer and hopes to one day ask her about.
“But yes, she is a very charming and beautiful woman,” he emphasized.
Instagram users weighed in on his reaction in the comments section, with one writing the following response: “If she doesn’t want him, I’ll take him 😁.”
Another joked, writing, “Just tell her you love her already! 😩😍😍😍😍.”
During the interview, Nienow congratulated Shakira on her upcoming World Cup performance.
“It’s a source of Latino pride. You’re one of the artists who will perform at the World Cup final,” he said in Spanish, according to a TODAY.com translation.

Shakira said the opportunity to sing at the event has “even deeper meaning” for her and brings together her passions for education and music. The musician also spoke about her song “Dai Dai,” which she described as a “song with a purpose.”

“I’ll not only be able to perform at the World Cup, which is obviously an incredible event for everyone, an event that awakens all kinds of passions. But we’ll also be able to achieve so much for children who are currently outside the educational system and who don’t even have access to a quality education,” she said (translated from Spanish).
CORRECTION 5/19 at 1:06: This article originally stated the network was Univision. It is Telemundo.

title: What Is Emily Henry Reading This Summer?
summary: Emily Henry comes to TODAY to share four of her book recommendations with picks by Kennedy Ryan, Mary Kubica, kylie Lee Baker and more.
story: Emily Henry is known for the kind of books that people love carting on vacation. But what are the books that she likes reading?
Henry stopped by TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle on May 19 to share a few of her book recommendations.

While she’s known for writing romance, Henry is a fan of thrillers. The last time she recommended books on TODAY, she shouted out “A Love Song for Ricki Wilde” by Tia Williams, “Darling Girls” by Sally Hepworth and “The Ministry of Time” by Kaliane Bradley.
This time, she has a blend of romance and genre-bending thrillers.
"Score" by Kennedy Ryan
The second novel in Kennedy Ryan’s “Hollywood Renaissance” series is the story of second chances, mental health diagnoses and the making of a movie.

Verity Hill and Monk Bellamy fell in love in college but their romance ended due to a traumatic misunderstanding. They’re hired, years later, to work together on a movie reviving a legend from Black history, and their own history comes out.
"It's Not Her" by Mary Kubica

Thriller writer Mary Kubica turns her sight to a family vacation gone tragically awry. Courtney Gray finds her brother and sister-in-law dead; her niece missing and her nephew unharmed. Where does anyone go from here?
"The Take" by Kelly Yang

Maggie Wang is a broke writer who accepts a questionable bargain. She agrees to a blood transfusion with Ingrid Parker, a veteran white Hollywood producer, who gives her $4 million for 10 sessions in the attempt to reverse her aging. What else is Maggie giving up?
"Japanese Gothic" by Kylie Lee Baker

The word to describe this thriller is ambitious. In present day New York, a college student kills his roommate and doesn’t know why. In 19th century Japan, a female samurai worries after her father comes back from war ... different. The book blends Japanese mythology and time travel with the propulsiveness of a murder mystery.

title: Sam Heughan Shares His Theory About What Happened to Jamie in the ‘Outlander’ Finale
summary: Sam Heughan shares his theory of what happened to Jamie at the end of OUtlander. He’s not sure if his character survived.
story: Sam Heughan knows “Outlander” fans are wondering what happened to his character, Jamie, after that series finale, and he has thoughts.
The Starz series ended its eight-season run on May 15 with a finale that saw Jamie getting shot (and presumably killed) by Major Patrick Ferguson during a battle.
Earlier on in the season, Jamie read a book from a future and learned he was fated to die in the battle. He, along with viewers, likely assumed he was a goner.

But the end of the finale complicates things. Claire (Caitriona Balfe) sits with Jamie on the battlefield after he’s been shot. Her hair turns bright white then and there — seeming to confirm a prophecy that said when her hair turned white, she would come into her healing powers.
The episode ends with Jamie and Claire gasping for air. Was Claire able to heal her husband?
In an interview with Entertainment Weekly, Heughan was asked to share his interpretation of the finale.

“I don’t know if I fully understand it. There was, I believe, an element of Claire having a power. It goes back to her being la dame blanche, this white witch. She brings the child back to life earlier. There’s the Faith of it all. Her hair changes color using her special ability,” he said.
Heughan then said he thinks the writers “changed their minds” on how to present the story.

“In my head, or the way I’m interpreting it, is it’s really up to the fans, whether they’re alive again together. They’re, of course, always together. But are they alive in this world or in the next?” he said.
If Heughan had his way, the characters would be “alive together.”
“I think them even waking up on the standing stone itself might signify that perhaps they’re somewhere else,” he said.
If Jamie and Claire are alive, Heughan said he hopes they’re in a “nice, peaceful place.”
“They’ve done their battles. They’ve made amends. For me, I think the story stops there,” he said.
Heughan told The Hollywood Reporter that the ending was originally more clear-cut.

“Initially, they wanted to make it quite clear that she used her power to bring him back to life. But, actually, it’s more ambiguous the way it’s been presented to us, and we don’t really know whether they’re alive or perhaps somewhere else,” he said.
In his mind, they’re together — either on the Earth or in the great beyond.

“We’re lying on the main standing stone that’s been through with us, that’s transported Claire through time. It is cracked down the middle; it’s broken. So yeah, that journey is probably over. So maybe they’re together alive on Fraser’s Ridge, or maybe they’re together somewhere else eternally,” he continued.
What’s important is that the characters are together in some capacity.
“I think that’s why season eight is quite magical. Even the (number) eight, which is the infinity sign. I think they’re together forever,” he said.

title: Joe Fryer Cuts Off His Beard for the First Time in 9 Years. See the Results – and His Reaction!
summary: Joe Fryer showed off his hair transformation. See how he shaved his beard and what happened in the before and after.
story: Saturday TODAY’s Joe Fryer is sporting a fresh-faced new look, just in time for summer.
After rocking his signature mustache and beard for nine years straight, Fryer decided it was time for a change, and he took viewers behind the scenes of his transformation
In a video posted to TODAY’s Instagram account, Fryer and his colleague, meteorologist Angie Lassman, stopped by Barberino’s at Rockefeller Center to visit with some grooming pros.
“I can just tell you I’m not getting a haircut,” Fryer said at the beginning of the video.
“Oh, what might you be doing?” Lassman asked.
“Well, I’m actually going to be cutting off my beard entirely for the first time in nine years,” Fryer replied while rubbing his beard.
Lassman was excited to witness all the action firsthand and said she was ready to see “Joe Fryer 2.0.”
The scene then shifted to Fryer lying down in one of the groomer’s chairs with a towel over his eyes.
“Now I see why you wanted to come here,” Lassman joked.
“See, this is really rough. This is tough,” Fryer replied.
After the Barberino’s team shaved off Fryer’s beard, he got a glimpse of what he looked like with just a mustache.
“Oh yea, Joe,” Lassman teased.
“Is this what we’re doing?” he asked.
When it came time for the big reveal, Fryer was taken aback by his bare skin and said, “Oh my goodness!”
“Are you a new man?” Lassman asked.
“That is what my chin looks like. There’s my upper lip. Wow!” Fryer said.
